$comment: Source of truth for the apodictic:story_spine block — the PRODUCER for Viz Chart 7-fiction (beat-map-against-spine; docs/manuscript-visualizations.md §The chart set, chart 7-fiction). ONE block per manuscript. It records the manuscript's chosen fiction SPINE FRAMEWORK and its ORDERED BEATS, each beat mapped to a Timeline scene. GROUNDING: the plot-architecture skill (plot-coach) names a CLOSED taxonomy of 50 named spines in 12 families (skills/plot-architecture/SKILL.md §Spine Families (50 Spines, 12 Families)), and each spine carries its OWN named, ordered beats in its 'On the page' line (references/plot-architecture-audit.md) — e.g. Seven-Point (Dan Wells): Hook -> Plot Turn 1 -> Pinch 1 -> Midpoint -> Pinch 2 -> Plot Turn 2 -> Resolution; Kishotenketsu: Ki -> Sho -> Ten -> Ketsu; Save the Cat: inciting incident -> midpoint flip -> all-is-lost -> finale. This block copies that taxonomy: `spine_framework` is a token in the CLOSED 50-spine set (a value outside it is a schema failure — an invented framework plot-coach never names); `beat` is that framework's named beat, kept a FREE non-empty string because beats are open PER framework and are not exhaustively enumerated (the scene_roster.v1 character-name precedent — a name/beat is author-declared, made auditable by its anchor, not enum-closed). The `beats[]` order IS the spine order the render draws. This reading is PRODUCER/AUTHOR-ENFORCED (a validator cannot read prose to adjudicate WHICH spine a manuscript follows or WHICH beat a scene is — the scene_roster.v1 / scene_function.v1 / tension_point.v1 author-enforced precedent) and made ACCOUNTABLE by the REQUIRED, non-empty `anchor` (a Timeline-relative line-range or a short on-page quote witnessing the beat). VERBATIM-COPY DISCIPLINE: the manifest's beats[] entries are bare {beat, scene_id} copied from these beats and byte-checked against them (viz_manifest.py X9); the renderer draws a beat-map along the spine (beats in producer/manifest order, each mapped to its scene) MECHANICALLY and invents nothing. PROVENANCE-CLOSED: every scene_id must resolve to a Timeline Event-Ledger row. SCOPE NOTE: beats are keyed directly on scene_id (per-scene, like scene_roster/scene_function/tension_point) — no evidence_ref -> scene_id resolution is needed or performed here. additionalProperties:false — a stray top-level key is a typo. The nested beats[] objects are validated by viz_manifest.py (the subset engine does not recurse). The GATE enforces schema + the closed 50-spine framework enum + beat-non-empty + anchor-non-empty + provenance closure, NOT the semantic beat reading. Validated by scripts/viz_manifest.py (X9). See docs/manuscript-visualizations.md.
title: APODICTIC story spine (apodictic.story_spine.v1)
